title: Unlimited Tools MCP bundle distribution channel (E23 design)
description: DESIGN (E23, docs/mcp-bundle-distribution.md): a named, ordered publish history for signed profile bundles (docs/mcp-signed-profile-bundles.md), owned by one issuer key. A channel is a local JSON FILE distributed over any transport (git repo, shared drive, future registry sync as a dumb carrier); it routes members to bundle file SHA-256s and grants NO capability by itself -- every routed bundle still passes the unchanged E14 verification against the member's E15 trust store, fail-closed with the reserved codes (-32014..-32019). Channel identity is the pair (name, owner.key_id); 'revision' is monotonic and consumers refuse to move to a lower revision (anti-replay). Exactly one history record has status 'active' and 'current' must equal its bundle_sha256 (semantic load checks, with non-decreasing published_at and signature.key_id equal to owner.key_id when signed -- enforced by the loader/tests, not this schema). The signature envelope is OPTIONAL in the format (MIT core may use unsigned channels locally, decision 1) and REQUIRED for registered/business-tier distribution; when present it is the exact E13 envelope over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document minus 'signature'. History 'revoked' status is informational routing state only; authoritative revocation stays the E15 CRL. NOTHING in E23 is implemented or hosted; this schema fixes the file contract only.

name.description: Operator-facing channel name (stable, beta, team-reviewers). Opaque: no semantics are parsed out of it. Channel identity is (name, owner.key_id) -- a name alone can never be squatted.

revision.description: Monotonic document revision; every republication bumps it. Consumers remember the highest revision applied per channel identity and refuse to move to a lower one (the offline anti-replay rule, design threat 20).

owner.description: The channel's owning issuer. Trust decisions use key_id plus the member's local trusted-keys file ONLY; display is for messages and grants nothing.

key_id.description: Opaque identifier of the owning/signing key (the E13 key_id grammar); must equal signature.key_id when the document is signed (semantic load check). Never a key value.

display.description: Human-readable owner name for messages. Never used for trust decisions.

history.description: Append-only publish history, oldest first, published_at non-decreasing (semantic load check). Exactly one record has status 'active' (semantic load check). A sha256 may appear more than once: rollback re-publishes a prior sha as a NEW active record at a higher document revision -- history is never rewritten or deleted.

bundle_sha256.description: SHA-256 (lowercase hex) of the signed bundle file bytes -- the same content address the E20 library and the E15 CRL use.

published_at.description: When this record was published, RFC 3339 UTC ('Z' only). Non-decreasing across the array (semantic load check).

status.description: Routing state: 'active' (the single current record), 'superseded' (replaced by a later record), 'revoked' (do not activate -- informational; authoritative revocation stays the E15 CRL, which member-side verification enforces regardless, -32017 bundle_revoked).

current.description: The current pointer: must equal the bundle_sha256 of the single history record with status 'active' (semantic load check) -- the pointer and the history can never disagree.

signature.description: OPTIONAL detached-signature envelope (decision 1: MIT core may distribute unsigned channels because routing files grant no capability; the registered/business tier REQUIRES it). Exact E13 envelope shape, computed over the canonical JSON (UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace) of this document with the 'signature' member removed. key_id must equal owner.key_id (semantic load check).

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm. Fixed to ed25519 like every signature envelope in this stack; widening the enum requires a new reviewed design.

key_id.description: Identifier of the verification key in the member's local trusted-keys file; must equal owner.key_id (semantic load check). Never a key value.
